title: Model STM32WL RNG in Renode
description: Discovered while validating project-LICHEN-5ix1.5. Repaired puck firmware no longer watchdog-resets, but main blocks pending on a semaphore inside entropy_stm32_rng_data because the Nucleo Renode platform has no peripheral at STM32WL RNG address 0x58001000. Renode 1.16.1 provides Miscellaneous.STM32F4_RNG and maps st,stm32-rng to it; generated devicetree uses IRQ 52. Add and validate the model so puck reaches its application late oracle.
acceptance_criteria: 1. Nucleo Renode platform models RNG at 0x58001000 with IRQ 52. 2. Canonical lora_ping and gateway regressions remain passing. 3. Canonical puck exits entropy wait, logs application/watchdog startup, and reaches an app-valid late oracle. 4. Exact artifacts/logs are retained. 5. Three independent code reviews find no correctness regression.
notes: Final 2026-07-16: added Renode built-in Miscellaneous.STM32F4_RNG at STM32WL address 0x58001000 with NVIC IRQ 52. Added `rng IRQ Set false` to canonical reset macro because Renode 1.16.1 STM32F4_RNG.Reset does not deassert its level IRQ. Runtime with existing clean ELFs: lora_ping simulator observed one 4-byte TX; gateway reached full L2 startup and `CoAP server on port 5683 (AUTOSTART)`; puck reached full L2 startup, `LICHEN puck starting`, `watchdog armed (4000 ms...)`, `L2/CoAP mode`, and simulator observed four transmissions, with no watchdog reset. Direct canonical reset macro invocation succeeded after forcing RNG IRQ high. Logs retained under /var/folders/pg/t21pc_0j6_3bq40d087mjbrw0000gn/T/opencode/fix-now-nucleo-runtime and final-lora-ping-nucleo-*. Three independent final reviews found no blocking defects. STM32WL HTCR/error fidelity is outside current configs; automated firmware/reset coverage tracked separately.
close_reason: Modeled STM32WL RNG and cleared stale IRQ on reset; all three app regressions and three reviews pass.
